Introduction to Web Application Security

Web application security is a critical field in modern cybersecurity. As organizations move more services online, the attack surface grows. This course introduces core concepts every security professional must know.

The OWASP Top 10

The OWASP Top 10 is a standard awareness document for developers and security teams. It represents the most critical security risks to web applications. Injection flaws, broken authentication, and sensitive data exposure top the list.

SQL Injection

SQL injection occurs when untrusted user input is concatenated into database queries. An attacker can read, modify, or delete data. Parameterized queries are the defense.

Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)

XSS allows attackers to inject client-side scripts into web pages viewed by other users. Stored XSS, reflected XSS, and DOM-based XSS are the three main categories.
